text:
A long time ago in a forest filled with towering trees, there lived an odd little creature named Mittens. Mittens didn't have paws; she had tiny wing-like things that allowed her to flutter gracefully from branch to branch. Mittens was the friendliest little being you could imagine. Every morning, she would take off her tiny hat with ear rims and flap her wings to greet the dewy morning sunshine.

Every week, Mittens would fly to the village at the edge of the forest to deliver colorful berries that grew deep in the forest's heart. The villagers adored these berries, even though they were not quite as sweet as ones from actual berries.

One particular week, Mittens missed her usual flight because the forest had a strange quietness. She had been visited by the Old Owl, a wise and somewhat mysterious old bird, who had told Mittens about a mysterious cloud hanging in the sky. According to Old Owl, it was a cloud that could grant one wish, but
